intro: Rubus leptadenes, also known as the Asian raspberry or thimbleberry, is a species of thorny shrub that belongs to the genus Rubus. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rubus leptadenes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus leptadenes include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
